entries.name: Tacit and unstructured
entries.description: The resource exists but its contents are not described in any retrievable way.
entries.keyCharacteristics: No labels, no index, no searchable description.
entries.accessibilityConsideration: Resource may exist only in personal possession or memory.
entries.licensingConsideration: Not meaningful at this level. No licensing question arises until the resource is described and potentially shared.

entries.name: Locally described
entries.description: The resource is labelled, dated, located, but the description is informal and local.
entries.keyCharacteristics: Free-text labels, dates, locations, no shared structure, no documented schema.
entries.accessibilityConsideration: Resource and description may be on personal devices or storage with limited access.
entries.licensingConsideration: Rarely addressed at this level. Resources are typically held personally or within a team without explicit re-use terms.
entries.branchOrJumpNotes: Description without documentation of conventions is not yet T03 even if structured.
entries.whatItEnables: Local retrieval by the original researcher or close colleagues.

entries.name: Structured to a documented local schema
entries.description: Description follows a consistent local convention with explicit documentation of the schema.
entries.keyCharacteristics: Equivalent fields in equivalent places, schema documented and usable by others, required and optional fields distinguishable.
entries.accessibilityConsideration: Schema and resource should be at minimum institutionally accessible to count as useful at this level.
entries.licensingConsideration: Licensing becomes meaningful once the schema is documented and the data could in principle be shared. Often still implicit, but explicit licensing increasingly expected by funders.
entries.populationCompletenessNotes: Required, recommended, and optional fields become distinguishable from this level upward.
entries.typicallyRequiresNotes: T02 plus documentation of the schema.
entries.branchOrJumpNotes: Required versus optional distinction becomes meaningful from this level upward.
entries.whatItEnables: Project-level consistency, basic querying, onboarding of new team members.

entries.name: Structured to a shared schema (informal or emerging)
entries.description: Schema is shared across organisations but not yet widely adopted or formally endorsed.
entries.keyCharacteristics: Multi-institutional usage, evolving conventions, schema documented and openly available.
entries.accessibilityConsideration: Schema openly accessible, resources may still be in restricted storage.
entries.licensingConsideration: Clear re-use terms increasingly expected as resources begin to be exchanged. Schema itself should also carry a clear licence.
entries.branchOrJumpNotes: Schema in active development, may shift, early-adopter community.
entries.whatItEnables: Limited but real exchange with partners using the same schema.

entries.name: Structured to an accepted shared schema
entries.description: Schema is community-endorsed and stable, whether or not it carries formal Standard status.
entries.keyCharacteristics: Aligned to widely adopted conventions such as Spectrum, LIDO, aggregator schemas, formal or de facto standards.
entries.accessibilityConsideration: Schema openly accessible and resources increasingly expected to be too.
entries.licensingConsideration: Explicit machine-readable re-use licences expected. Aggregators and community infrastructures typically require declared licences for inclusion.
entries.typicallyRequiresNotes: T04a or direct adoption of an established schema.
entries.branchOrJumpNotes: This is where genuine interoperability begins to be realistic across the field.
entries.whatItEnables: Reliable exchange across the community using the schema, foundation for aggregator participation.

entries.name: Aligned to standard published vocabularies
entries.description: Field values are mapped to external, published, reusable vocabularies.
entries.keyCharacteristics: Terms resolve to AAT, Wikidata, domain thesauri, either directly or through documented mapping from local terms.
entries.licensingConsideration: Vocabulary licences themselves need to be compatible with the planned re-use. Most major vocabularies are openly licensed.
entries.populationCompletenessNotes: Applies to which fields are vocabulary-controlled.
entries.typicallyRequiresNotes: T03 or higher, can occur independently of T06.
entries.branchOrJumpNotes: May precede or follow T06, often happens in parallel.
entries.whatItEnables: Cross-language and cross-collection retrieval, semantic disambiguation.

entries.name: Bearing persistent identifiers (PIDs)
entries.description: Resources and entities have publicly resolvable persistent identifiers.
entries.keyCharacteristics: DOIs, IGSNs, ORCIDs, ROR, ARKs, Handles, with registered metadata at the PID authority.
entries.accessibilityConsideration: PIDs resolvable globally by definition, though resources behind them may have access restrictions.
entries.licensingConsideration: PID metadata at registration time typically includes a licence statement. Resources behind PIDs without clear licences create well-known reusability gaps.
entries.populationCompletenessNotes: Applies to which entities bear PIDs.
entries.typicallyRequiresNotes: T03 or higher, can occur independently of T05.
entries.branchOrJumpNotes: May precede or follow T05, often happens in parallel.
entries.whatItEnables: Unambiguous external referencing, durable citation, foundation for federation.

entries.name: Rich PID-bearing metadata datasets
entries.description: T06 reached with substantial optional content, contextual links to raw data, methods, and related resources.
entries.keyCharacteristics: Comprehensive coverage, broad context captured at metadata level, no semantic layer yet.
entries.accessibilityConsideration: Resources increasingly openly accessible or with documented access conditions.
entries.licensingConsideration: Re-use licences expected to be present and machine-readable. This is the level at which FAIR reusability becomes a realistic claim.
entries.populationCompletenessNotes: High level of completeness reached by definition at this level.
entries.typicallyRequiresNotes: T06 with rich population, typically also T04b and T05.
entries.branchOrJumpNotes: Major branch point: continue along metadata-based trajectory or move to knowledge-graph trajectory.
entries.whatItEnables: Legitimate stopping point for many projects, FAIR datasets at metadata level.

entries.name: Semantically represented (ad hoc)
entries.description: Structure is expressed against a formal ontology but without consistent application.
entries.keyCharacteristics: CIDOC CRM or extensions used, but modelling choices undocumented, locally invented, or inconsistent across the dataset.
entries.accessibilityConsideration: Resource access may be open but semantic interoperability is still limited.
entries.licensingConsideration: As for T04b. Licensing of the semantic representation itself should be clear and compatible with re-use as linked data.
entries.typicallyRequiresNotes: T03 or higher, plus ontology application.
entries.branchOrJumpNotes: Common in early adoption, limits real interoperability even though formally semantic.

entries.name: Semantically represented against recommended implementations
entries.description: Ontology applied through documented, community-endorsed modelling patterns.
entries.keyCharacteristics: CIDOC CRM, CRMsci, CRMdig applied via recommended implementations, with modelling choices documented and consistent.
entries.accessibilityConsideration: Resource and representation increasingly expected to be openly accessible.
entries.licensingConsideration: Open licences strongly preferred to enable linked data use. Recommended implementations themselves should carry clear licences.
entries.typicallyRequiresNotes: T04b plus ontology plus recommended implementations.
entries.branchOrJumpNotes: Automated tooling can lift T04b/T06+ resources directly to T07b without manual semantic modelling.
entries.whatItEnables: Genuine semantic interoperability, machine-actionable relationships, reliable inference.

entries.name: Linked and federated (metadata-level)
entries.description: Rich metadata representations are published, addressable, and connected across institutions, without full semantic layer.
entries.keyCharacteristics: Resolvable URIs, linked data publication of metadata, federation through aggregators or registries.
entries.accessibilityConsideration: Resources openly accessible or with documented access conditions.
entries.licensingConsideration: Open machine-readable licences essentially required for federation. Aggregator participation typically gated on explicit licensing.
entries.branchOrJumpNotes: Continuation of metadata-based trajectory at federated scale.
entries.whatItEnables: Cross-institutional discovery and reuse of metadata, FAIR metadata at scale.

entries.name: Linked and federated (semantic)
entries.description: Semantic representations are published, addressable, and connected across institutions.
entries.keyCharacteristics: Resolvable URIs, linked data publication of semantic representations, federation with reasoning support.
entries.accessibilityConsideration: Resources openly accessible or with documented access conditions.
entries.licensingConsideration: Open machine-readable licences essentially required. Mixed-licence federated graphs raise well-known practical and legal issues.
entries.branchOrJumpNotes: Full semantic federation, may include or link to raw and processed data.
entries.whatItEnables: Genuine FAIR data, cross-institutional inference and reasoning.

entries.name: Integrated within a documented digital construct
entries.description: Resources brought together as a planned, documented structure with explicit purpose, commonly described as a Digital Twin.
entries.keyCharacteristics: Documented specification for what is integrated and why, including metadata, data, methods, interpretation, planned use cases.
entries.accessibilityConsideration: Open access increasingly expected for the construct as a whole.
entries.licensingConsideration: Licensing of the integrated construct must reconcile the licences of its components. Mixed-licence constructs need explicit handling.
entries.branchOrJumpNotes: May involve further sub-levels as Digital Twin practice matures, definitions still evolving.
entries.whatItEnables: Coherent research objects, foundation for advanced analytical workflows and reuse.
